data.title: Kimberly-Clark leads all large-cap consumer staples stocks in YoY CapEx growth
data.description:
A rising capital expenditure figure often signals that a company is betting heavily on its own future growth. Below is a list of U.S. large-cap consumer staples stocks ranked by their year-over-year CapEx growth percentage, spanning multiple sectors and market capitalizations.

The list spans multiple sub-sectors, including Household Products, Tobacco, Consumer Staples Merchandise Retail, Agricultural Products & Services, Food Retail, and Food Distributors.

Kimberly-Clark (KMB) tops the list with a CapEx growth rate of 57.84% year-over-year, as the company accelerates what it has called the largest transformation in its 150-year history under its "Powering Care" strategy, reinvesting aggressively in manufacturing capabilities and brand innovation after removing $200 million in structural costs through its 2024 restructuring program.

Altria Group (MO) and Target Corporation (TGT) follow in second and third place with growth rates of 52.11% and 28.92% respectively, while Bunge Global (BG) and Casey's General Stores (CASY) round out the top five at 25.22% and 23.72%, with Bunge projecting $1.5 to $1.7 billion in full-year capital expenditures for 2026 as it scales its global agri-processing operations.

Procter & Gamble (PG) and US Foods (USFD) occupy the sixth and seventh positions with CapEx growth of 23.09% and 20.23% respectively, US Foods deploying $410 million in cash CapEx in fiscal 2025 as part of a plan to deploy over $4 billion in capital from 2025 to 2027.

BJ's Wholesale Club (BJ) and Costco (COST) follow at 19.40% and 17.30%, while Walmart (WMT) rounds out the list at 12.02%. 

Here is the list:

  1. Kimberly-Clark Corporation (KMB), CapEx growth YoY: 57.84%

  2. Altria Group, Inc. (MO), CapEx growth YoY: 52.11%

  3. Target Corporation (TGT), CapEx growth YoY: 28.92%

  4. Bunge Global SA (BG), CapEx growth YoY: 25.22%

  5. Casey's General Stores, Inc. (CASY), CapEx growth YoY: 23.72%

  6. The Procter & Gamble Company (PG), CapEx growth YoY: 23.09%

  7. US Foods Holding Corp. (USFD), CapEx growth YoY: 20.23%

  8. BJ's Wholesale Club Holdings, Inc. (BJ), CapEx growth YoY: 19.40%

  9. Costco Wholesale Corporation (COST), CapEx growth YoY: 17.30%

  10. Walmart Inc. (WMT), CapEx growth YoY: 12.02%

Consumer Staples ETFs: (XLP), (VDC), (IYK), (FSTA), (KXI), and (RSPS)

data.title: Canada to launch its first sovereign wealth fund with initial C$25B funding
data.description:
Canada will launch its first sovereign wealth fund, with an initial federal contribution of C$25B ($18.3B) over three years, Prime Minister Mark Carney announced as part of a broader effort to build a more resilient and independent economy.

The Canada Strong Fund will invest, alongside the private sector, in Canadian projects and companies across sectors including clean energy, critical minerals, agriculture and infrastructure. The returns will be reinvested to grow the fund.

"Many countries that are blessed with natural resources like Norway have sovereign wealth funds," Carney said. "Canada hasn't had one, until now."

"And if you have a bit of extra money, we'll make it easy for you to invest in the fund to help build Canada strong for all," he added.

A retail investment product will be launched to provide Canadians the option to invest and share in the returns. The government will consult on the design of this new instrument over the coming months. 

The Canada Strong Fund will operate at arm's length from the government, with its work guided by a CEO and an independent board of directors. 

Opposition Leader Pierre Poilievre criticized the move, calling it a sovereign debt fund. "Norway, Singapore and Saudi Arabia run big budget surpluses, which they accumulate and put into their sovereign wealth funds," he said. "Carney has no surplus and therefore no wealth to put in such a fund."

data.title: Allegion Non-GAAP EPS of $1.80 misses by $0.10, revenue of $1.03B in-line
data.description:
• Allegion press release (ALLE): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.80 misses by $0.10.
  • Revenue of $1.03B (+9.4% Y/Y) in-line.
  • First-quarter 2026 net revenues increased 9.7%. On an organic basis, which excludes impacts of acquisitions, divestitures and foreign currency movements, net revenues increased 2.6%, led by the Americas region. The organic revenue increase was driven by price realization partially offset by volume declines. Reported revenue reflects a 4.8% net positive impact from acquisitions and divestitures, as well as a 2.3% tailwind from foreign currency.

  • The company is raising its full-year 2026 reported revenue growth outlook to a range of 6% to 8%, inclusive of the recent DCI acquisition, and is affirming its organic growth outlook of 2% to 4%, after excluding the expected impacts of acquisitions, divestitures and foreign currency movements.

    The company is affirming the outlook for full-year 2026 adjusted EPS to be in the range of $8.70 to $8.90 vs $8.80 consensus. The company is updating the outlook for full-year 2026 reported EPS and expects it to be in the range of $7.95 to $8.15. Adjustments to 2026 EPS include estimated impacts of approximately $0.60 per share for acquisition-related amortization, as well as $0.15 per share for restructuring and M&A.

    The outlook continues to assume a full-year adjusted effective tax rate of approximately 18% to 19%.

    The outlook continues to assume an average diluted share count for the full year of approximately 86.6 million shares.

    The company still expects full-year available cash flow to be 85% to 95% of adjusted net income.

data.title: Hilton Worldwide reports mixed results; introduces Q2 and updates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Hilton Worldwide press release (HLT): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.01 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $2.94B (+8.9% Y/Y) misses by $10M.

  • Outlook: Full Year 2026

    • System-wide comparable RevPAR, on a currency neutral basis, is projected to increase between 2.0 percent and 3.0 percent compared to 2025.
    • Diluted EPS is projected to be between $8.28 and $8.40.
    • Diluted EPS, adjusted for special items, is projected to be between $8.79 and $8.91 vs. $9.05 consensus  (prior $8.65 and $8.77).
    • Net income is projected to be between $1,909 million and $1,937 million.
    • Adjusted EBITDA is projected to be between $4,020 million and $4,060 million.
    • Contract acquisition costs and capital expenditures, excluding amounts reimbursed by third parties, are projected to be approximately $300 million.
    • Capital return is projected to be approximately $3.5 billion.
    • General and administrative expenses are projected to be approximately $400 million.
    • Net unit growth is projected to be between 6.0 percent and 7.0 percent.

    Second Quarter 2026

    • System-wide comparable RevPAR, on a currency neutral basis, is projected to increase between 2.0 percent and 3.0 percent compared to the second quarter of 2025.
    • Diluted EPS is projected to be between $2.13 and $2.19.
    • Diluted EPS, adjusted for special items, is projected to be between $2.18 and $2.24 vs. $2.43 consensus.
    • Net income is projected to be between $491 million and $505 million.
    • Adjusted EBITDA is projected to be between $1,015 million and $1,035 million.
    • Projected second quarter year over year growth rates for profitability measures are impacted by one-time fees and favorable timing items specific to the second quarter of 2025, as well as the outsized impact of anticipated lower Middle East RevPAR.

data.title: Spotify GAAP EPS of $3.45 beats by $0.51, revenue of $4.53B beats by $10M
data.description:
• Spotify press release (SPOT): Q1 GAAP EPS of $3.45 beats by $0.51.
  • Revenue of $4.53B (+7.9% Y/Y) beats by $10M.
  • Premium Revenue growth of 10% Y/Y (or 15% Y/Y constant currency*), driven by subscriber gains; and
  •  Ad-Supported Revenue decline of 5% Y/Y (or increase of 3% Y/Y constant currency*)
  • Unfavorable currency movements slowed Total Revenue Y/Y growth by ~600 bps vs. the ~670 bpsincorporated into our guidance
  • Q1 MAU net adds of 10 milliondrove total MAUs to 761 million,accelerating to 12% Y/Y growth
  • Premium Subscribers grew 9% Y/Yto 293 million, reflecting 3 millionquarterly net adds.
  • Shares -8% PM.

data.title: Centene beats Q1 street views
data.description:
• Centene press release (CNC): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $3.37 beats by $1.24.
  • Revenue of $49.94B (+7.1% Y/Y) beats by $2.39B.
  • Medicaid HBR of 93.1%, reflecting continued tangible progress managing medical costs coupled with moderate flu.
  • Medicare segment HBR of 84.9%, from outperformance in both Medicare Advantage and PDP.

  • The Company is increasing its 2026 premium and service revenues guidance range by $1.0 billion to a range of $171.0 billion to $175.0 billion driven by Medicaid (vs. consensus of $188.8B). The Company is also increasing its investment and other income expectation by $50 million to $1.45 billion.

    The Company is updating its 2026 GAAP diluted EPS guidance floor to greater than $2.37 and its 2026 adjusted diluted EPS guidance floor to greater than $3.40. (vs. consensus of $3.02)

data.title: Enterprise Products Partners GAAP EPS of $0.68 misses by $0.03, revenue of $14.39B beats by $770M
data.description:
• Enterprise Products Partners press release (EPD): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.68 misses by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $14.39B (-6.7% Y/Y) beats by $770M.
  • Operational DCF: $2.1 billion, which provided 1.8x coverage of distributions declared for 1Q 2026, up 5%; retained $1.5 billion of DCF
  • Distributions declared: $0.55 per common unit, or $2.20 per common unit annualized, up 2.8%
  •

data.title: EU regulator proposes measures to Google for rival AI services on Android
data.description:
The European Commission said it sent draft measures to Google, which the Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL) unit should implement to ensure that third parties, such as online search rivals and AI developers, have effective access and interoperability with key capabilities of Android.

The Commission noted that the proposed measures aim to ensure that competing AI services can effectively interact with applications on users' Android devices and execute tasks accordingly, such as sending an email using the user's preferred email app.

"Today’s proposed measures will give more choice to Android users about the AI services they use and integrate in their phone, including from the vast range of AI services that compete with Google’s own AI," said Teresa Ribera, European Commissioner for Competition and executive vice-president for a Clean, Just and Competitive Transition. 

Currently, Google largely reserves these capabilities for use by its own AI offerings on Android phones and tablets. For example, the measures would allow competing AI services to be easily activated by users, using a custom 'wake word,' a phrase that the user can speak to activate an AI service, the antitrust regulator added.

The proposed measures will also enable competing providers of AI services to innovate and offer integrated AI experiences to users on Android phones and tablets, along with Google' own AI services, such as Gemini, according to the Commission.

"Android’s open ecosystem enables AI assistants to thrive, as device makers have full autonomy to integrate and customise the AI experiences their users want. This unwarranted intervention would strip away that autonomy, mandate access to sensitive hardware and device permissions; unnecessarily driving up costs while undermining critical privacy and security protections for European users," said Clare Kelly, Senior Competition Counsel at Google, in an email to Seeking Alpha.

These preliminary findings are part of the proceedings the regulator started in January under the Digital Markets Act, or DMA.

Interested parties have until May 13 to submit their views on the draft measures. 

Earlier this month, the European Commission sent preliminary findings to Google outlining proposed measures under the DMA which the company should allow third-party search engines to access Google search data, including that of AI chatbots with search functionalities.

The DMA is a legislation that establishes a set of objective criteria to qualify a large online platform as a "gatekeeper" and ensure that they behave fairly online and leave room for contestability.

Update: The story was updated with a statement from Google.

data.title: Atlas Copco GAAP EPS of SEK 1.28, revenue of SEK 40.54B; gives near-term outlook
data.description:
• Atlas Copco press release (ATLKY): Q1 GAAP EPS of SEK 1.28.
  • Revenue of SEK 40.54B (-5.1% Y/Y).
  • Orders received reached MSEK 45 395 (46 604), organic increase of 5%.
  • Adjusted operating profit, excluding items affecting comparability, was MSEK 8,329.
  • Operating cash flow reached MSEK 4,355.
  • Near-term outlook: Atlas Copco Group expects the customer activity to remain at the current level.
  • Previous near-term outlook (published January 27, 2026): Atlas Copco Group expects that the customer activity will remain at the current level.

data.title: OpenAI growth headwinds ripple through partners; SoftBank and Oracle shares under pressure
data.description:
Shares in SoftBank Group (SFTBY) and Oracle (ORCL), both partners of OpenAI (OPENAI), fell in premarket trading on Tuesday after The Wall Street Journal reported the AI startup recently missed targets for sales and new users, fueling concerns about growth expectations across the AI sector ahead of earnings.

SoftBank tumbled as much as 11% in Tokyo, while Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) was down ~3%, CoreWeave (CRWV) fell 3.5%, Nvidia (NVDA) eased 1%, and Oracle Corp. (ORCL) lost 4.4%.

SoftBank, which owns approximately 11% of OpenAI, suffered its worst single-day percentage loss in six months.

While OpenAI has struck deals with dozens of firms, markets tend to focus on a smaller subset of major partners, including NvidiaNvidia (NVDA), SoftBank, Oracle, Microsoft (MSFT), Coreweave, and AMD, as investment proxies for the creator of ChatGPT.

Investors are closely watching for signs that tech companies remain committed to their previously announced plans for massive capital spending on building out AI infrastructure. “That’s what the market needs to see to keep the AI narrative intact,” Amanda Lyons, head of research at Energy Group Capital, told Bloomberg.

“The nuance is that it’s a narrow path: any hint of slowing spend would be taken negatively for the ecosystem, but a sharp step-up would likely raise questions around returns and sustainability.”

A basket of companies connected to OpenAI has underperformed peers significantly in recent months, rising by about 75% since the end of 2024 compared with gains of more than 300% for a similar group of Alphabet Inc.-tied (GOOG) (GOOGL) stocks, the report said.

The Sam Altman- led firm fell short of several monthly sales targets in 2026 after rival Anthropic PBC (ANTHRO) gained ground in the coding and enterprise markets, the Journal reported.
